signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.18; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Fed 3.75% (ON HOLD) vs BoJ 0.75% (ON HOLD) — 450bp Fed-BoJ carry differential. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7165; HV30 at 6.5%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 7.3% — options pricing a 0.8 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.08: mild put skew. CFTC LF net long +41,675 (week Apr 14), long%=72.0% — extreme long positioning. Retail 89% short; contrarian reversal risk. Watch for LF delta turning negative to shift the read.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Technical
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1741; CFTC LF net short −72, long%=50.0%. Price testing 1.1730 — key level; ECB 2% (ON HOLD) vs Fed 3.75% (ON HOLD) — 175bp spread favors USD. Watch for price breaking below 1.1730 to confirm a technical downtrend.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 4.50% vs Fed 3.75% — 75bp GBP carry premium supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28,426; AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. GBP/USD at 1.3515 mid-range; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Retail Sentiment
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5917; retail 35% long / 65% short. No extreme positioning; NZD/USD range-bound. Watch for a breakout above 0.6000 to shift the read.
